_meta.description: Proficiency reference scales for all 32 candidate languages. Levels 1-5 map consistently across all scales.

1.descriptor: Can understand and use familiar everyday expressions; can handle simple personal transactions with repetition and slow speech.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected text on familiar topics; can describe experiences and briefly give reasons or explanations.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently with native speakers on a wide range of topics; can produce clear, detailed text with some complexity.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently and spontaneously without obvious searching; uses language flexibly and effectively for professional purposes.

5.descriptor: Can understand virtually everything heard or read; expresses spontaneously, very fluently and precisely, conveying finer shades of meaning.

1.descriptor: Can understand and use familiar everyday expressions; can handle simple personal transactions with repetition.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected text on familiar topics; can describe experiences and briefly give reasons.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently on a wide range of topics; can produce clear, detailed text on a wide range of subjects.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently and spontaneously; uses language flexibly and effectively for social, academic and professional purposes.

5.descriptor: Can understand virtually everything with ease; expresses spontaneously, very fluently and precisely.

1.descriptor: Can understand and use familiar everyday expressions; handles basic transactions.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected text; can describe experiences and give brief reasons.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently; can produce clear, detailed text on a wide range of subjects.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently and spontaneously; uses language flexibly for professional purposes.

5.descriptor: Can understand virtually everything; expresses spontaneously, very fluently and precisely.

1.descriptor: Can understand and use familiar everyday expressions; handles basic transactions.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected text; can describe experiences and give brief reasons.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently; produces clear, detailed text on diverse subjects.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently and spontaneously; flexible, effective professional use.

5.descriptor: Virtually everything understood; expresses spontaneously, very fluently and precisely.

1.descriptor: Can understand and use basic Swedish for immediate needs; handles simple transactions.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected text on familiar topics; handles most everyday situations.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently on a wide range of topics; produces clear, detailed Swedish text.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently and spontaneously; uses Swedish flexibly for professional purposes.

5.descriptor: Virtually everything understood; expresses spontaneously, very fluently and precisely in Swedish.

no-NO.name: Norwegian (Norway)
no-NO.note: Default is Bokmål (nb). Nynorsk (nn) is a distinct written standard — mixing is an error.

1.descriptor: Can understand and use basic Norwegian for immediate needs; handles simple transactions.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected text; handles most everyday situations in Norwegian.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently; produces clear, detailed Norwegian text on a wide range of subjects.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently; uses Norwegian flexibly for professional purposes.

5.descriptor: Virtually everything understood; expresses spontaneously, very fluently and precisely in Norwegian.

da-DK.name: Danish (Denmark)
da-DK.authority: Council of Europe / Prøve i Dansk

1.descriptor: Can understand and use basic Danish; handles simple everyday transactions.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected text; handles most everyday situations in Danish.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently; produces clear, detailed Danish text on a wide range of subjects.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently; uses Danish flexibly for professional purposes.

5.descriptor: Virtually everything understood; expresses spontaneously, very fluently and precisely in Danish.

fi-FI.name: Finnish (Finland)
fi-FI.note: Finnish has no grammatical gender but requires vowel harmony and complex case system. YKI maps directly to CEFR levels.

1.descriptor: Can understand and use basic Finnish expressions; handles simple routine transactions.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected Finnish text; handles most everyday situations including case variation.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently in Finnish on a wide range of topics; handles complex case structures.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently in Finnish; flexible, effective professional use including complex inflection.

5.descriptor: Mastery of Finnish including idiomatic constructions, dialectal awareness, and complex morphology.

es-ES.name: Spanish (Spain / Peninsular)
es-ES.authority: Council of Europe / Instituto Cervantes DELE

1.descriptor: Can understand and use basic Spanish for immediate needs; handles simple transactions.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected text; handles most everyday situations in Peninsular Spanish.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently; produces clear, detailed text; handles subjunctive and complex syntax.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently and spontaneously; uses Spanish flexibly for professional purposes.

5.descriptor: Virtually everything understood; expresses spontaneously, very fluently and precisely in Spanish.

es-419.name: Spanish (Latin America — generic)
es-419.authority: Council of Europe / Instituto Cervantes DELE
es-419.note: Single code covering all Latin American variants. Sub-variant (es-MX, es-AR, es-CO, es-CL, etc.) unknown unless briefed. Flag regional marker mixing.

1.descriptor: Can understand and use basic Spanish for immediate needs; handles simple transactions.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected text; handles most everyday situations in Spanish.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently; produces clear, detailed text on a wide range of subjects.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently and spontaneously; uses Spanish flexibly for professional purposes.

5.descriptor: Virtually everything understood; expresses spontaneously, very fluently and precisely.

pt-PT.name: Portuguese (Portugal / European)
pt-PT.authority: Council of Europe / Universidade de Lisboa CAPLE

1.descriptor: Can understand and use basic European Portuguese for immediate needs.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected text in European Portuguese; handles most everyday situations.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently in PT-PT; produces clear, detailed text using European conventions.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently in PT-PT; flexible, effective professional use.

5.descriptor: Mastery of European Portuguese; native-like command including regional register awareness.

pt-BR.name: Portuguese (Brazil)
pt-BR.note: Celpe-Bras uses its own grading internally; CEFR equivalence is internationally recognized for PT-BR assessment.

1.descriptor: Can understand and use basic Brazilian Portuguese for immediate needs.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected text in Brazilian Portuguese; handles most everyday situations.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently in PT-BR; produces clear, detailed text using Brazilian conventions.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently in PT-BR; flexible, effective professional use.

5.descriptor: Mastery of Brazilian Portuguese; native-like command including regional and cultural nuance.

ru-RU.name: Russian (Russia)
ru-RU.authority: Ministry of Education of Russia — torfl.ru (free descriptor access)
ru-RU.note: TORFL (ТРКИ) levels map to CEFR: Elementary=A2, Basic=B1, TRKI-1=B2, TRKI-2=C1, TRKI-3/4=C2.

1.descriptor: Can understand and use basic Russian for immediate needs; handles simple routine situations.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected text in Russian; handles most everyday situations.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently; understands complex texts on concrete and abstract topics.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently and spontaneously; effective professional use of Russian.

5.descriptor: Near-native command of Russian; understands and produces complex texts on any topic.

pl-PL.name: Polish (Poland)
pl-PL.authority: Council of Europe / State Commission for Certification of Proficiency in Polish

1.descriptor: Can understand and use basic Polish for immediate needs; handles simple transactions.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected Polish text; handles most everyday situations.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently in Polish; produces clear, detailed text on a wide range of subjects.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently in Polish; flexible, effective professional use.

5.descriptor: Mastery of Polish; native-like command including complex case and aspect usage.

cs-CZ.name: Czech (Czech Republic)
cs-CZ.authority: Council of Europe / Charles University CCE

1.descriptor: Can understand and use basic Czech for immediate needs.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected text in Czech; handles most everyday situations.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently in Czech; produces clear, detailed text on a wide range of subjects.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently in Czech; flexible, effective professional use.

5.descriptor: Mastery of Czech; native-like command including complex morphology and aspect.

hu-HU.name: Hungarian (Hungary)
hu-HU.note: Hungarian uses ECL and ORIGO exams, both CEFR-aligned. Agglutinative morphology means errors are highly specific.

1.descriptor: Can understand and use basic Hungarian for immediate needs; handles simple transactions.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected text in Hungarian; handles most everyday situations.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently in Hungarian; produces clear text with correct case government.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently in Hungarian; flexible professional use including complex verb-prefix patterns.

5.descriptor: Mastery of Hungarian; native-like command including definite/indefinite conjugation and complex suffixation.

el-GR.name: Greek (Greece)
el-GR.authority: Council of Europe / Greek Ministry of Education KPG exam

1.descriptor: Can understand and use basic Modern Greek for immediate needs.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected text in Modern Greek; handles most everyday situations.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently; produces clear, detailed Greek text on a wide range of subjects.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently in Greek; flexible, effective professional use.

5.descriptor: Mastery of Modern Greek; native-like command including complex morphology and register awareness.

tr-TR.name: Turkish (Turkey)
tr-TR.authority: Council of Europe / Yunus Emre Institute TYS (CEFR-aligned)
tr-TR.note: Turkish agglutinative morphology — vowel harmony and suffix stacking are key error indicators.

1.descriptor: Can understand and use basic Turkish for immediate needs; handles simple transactions.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected text in Turkish; handles most everyday situations.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently in Turkish; produces clear, detailed text on a wide range of subjects.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently in Turkish; flexible, effective professional use including complex suffix chains.

5.descriptor: Mastery of Turkish; native-like command including idiomatic expressions and correct vowel harmony throughout.

uk-UA.name: Ukrainian (Ukraine)
uk-UA.note: Watch for Russisms (русизми) and surzhyk — Ukrainian-Russian mixed speech — as non-native markers.

1.descriptor: Can understand and use basic Ukrainian for immediate needs.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected text in Ukrainian; handles most everyday situations without Russisms.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently in standard Ukrainian; produces clear, detailed text free of surzhyk.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently in Ukrainian; flexible, effective professional use with correct case government.

5.descriptor: Mastery of Ukrainian; native-like command with full awareness of standard vs dialectal forms.

ro-RO.name: Romanian (Romania)
ro-RO.authority: Council of Europe / Romanian Ministry of Education

1.descriptor: Can understand and use basic Romanian for immediate needs.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected text in Romanian; handles most everyday situations.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently in Romanian; produces clear, detailed text on a wide range of subjects.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently in Romanian; flexible, effective professional use.

5.descriptor: Mastery of Romanian; native-like command including enclitic article and case agreement.

bg-BG.name: Bulgarian (Bulgaria)
bg-BG.authority: Council of Europe / Sofia University BLS

1.descriptor: Can understand and use basic Bulgarian for immediate needs.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected text in Bulgarian; handles most everyday situations.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently in Bulgarian; produces clear, detailed text on a wide range of subjects.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently in Bulgarian; flexible, effective professional use.

5.descriptor: Mastery of Bulgarian; native-like command including definite article forms and verbal aspect.

ar-AA.name: Arabic (Modern Standard + dialects)
ar-AA.authority: American Council on the Teaching of Foreign Languages — actfl.org (free guidelines)
ar-AA.note: ar-AA covers MSA (فصحى). Dialectal variant (Egyptian, Levantine, Gulf, Maghrebi) unknown unless briefed. Code-switching MSA/dialect is a key non-native marker.

1.descriptor: Can communicate minimally on very familiar topics using memorized words and fixed phrases in MSA.

2.descriptor: Can create with language; handle uncomplicated communicative tasks using basic MSA structures.

3.descriptor: Can narrate and describe in MSA using paragraph-length discourse; handles a variety of topics.

4.descriptor: Can discuss topics extensively, support opinions and hypothesize in MSA with high precision.

5.descriptor: Exceptional precision and cultural nuance in MSA; can operate across registers including formal oratory.

hi-IN.name: Hindi (India)
hi-IN.authority: American Council on the Teaching of Foreign Languages — actfl.org (free guidelines)
hi-IN.note: No single globally standardized Hindi proficiency test. ACTFL scale is the most widely recognized free reference.

1.descriptor: Can communicate minimally on very familiar topics using memorized Hindi words and phrases.

2.descriptor: Can create simple Hindi sentences; handles basic communicative tasks on familiar social topics.

3.descriptor: Can narrate and describe in Hindi using paragraph-length discourse; handles gender agreement and postpositions correctly.

4.descriptor: Can discuss topics extensively in Hindi; precision in case postpositions, verb conjugation, and register.

5.descriptor: Native-like Hindi command with cultural nuance; can write across formal registers.

zh-CN.name: Chinese Simplified (Mainland China)
zh-CN.authority: Hanban / Confucius Institute Headquarters — chinesetest.cn (free descriptors)
zh-CN.note: Classic HSK 1-6. New HSK 3.0 (2021) extends to 9 bands; classic HSK 6 remains the professional benchmark.

1.descriptor: Can understand and use very familiar everyday Chinese expressions; survival-level communication.

2.descriptor: Can communicate on familiar topics; basic professional communication possible.

3.descriptor: Can discuss a wide range of topics with reasonable fluency; handles complex characters.

4.descriptor: Can read newspapers and watch films without subtitles; professional-level Chinese.

5.descriptor: Can easily comprehend any written or spoken Chinese; expresses spontaneously with precision — near native.

zh-TW.name: Chinese Traditional (Taiwan)
zh-TW.authority: Mandarin Training Center, MTAC Taiwan — tocfl.tw (free descriptors)
zh-TW.note: Traditional characters expected. Taiwan-specific vocabulary conventions apply (軟體 not 软件, 手機 not 手机). TOCFL maps to CEFR.

1.descriptor: Can understand and use basic Traditional Chinese for immediate needs.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected Traditional Chinese text; handles most everyday situations.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently; produces clear, detailed Traditional Chinese text on a wide range of subjects.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently; flexible, effective professional use of Traditional Chinese.

5.descriptor: Mastery of Traditional Chinese; native-like command with Taiwan-specific register awareness.

zh-HK.name: Chinese Traditional (Hong Kong / Cantonese context)
zh-HK.authority: Mandarin Training Center, MTAC Taiwan — tocfl.tw (free descriptors, Traditional Chinese benchmark)
zh-HK.note: Traditional characters expected (same as zh-TW). HK context: Cantonese-specific vocabulary and sentence-final particles (囉, 喎, 咋, 囧) may appear and are legitimate in informal register. Simplified characters are an error here.

1.descriptor: Can understand and use basic Traditional Chinese for immediate needs; may use Cantonese-influenced phrasing.

2.descriptor: Can produce simple connected Traditional Chinese text in HK conventions.

3.descriptor: Can interact fluently; produces clear Traditional Chinese text with awareness of HK/Cantonese distinctions.

4.descriptor: Can express ideas fluently; effective professional use of Traditional Chinese with HK register awareness.

5.descriptor: Mastery of Traditional Chinese in HK context; native-like command with full Cantonese/Mandarin register control.

ja-JP.name: Japanese (Japan)
ja-JP.authority: Japan Foundation / Japan Educational Exchanges and Services — jlpt.jp (free descriptors)

1.descriptor: Can understand some basic Japanese; can read hiragana, katakana and basic kanji; handles simple transactions.

2.descriptor: Can understand basic Japanese used in everyday situations; can read simple texts.

3.descriptor: Can understand Japanese in everyday situations to a certain degree; can follow conversations on familiar topics.

4.descriptor: Can understand Japanese in a broad range of situations; can follow native-speaker conversations and read newspapers.

5.descriptor: Can understand Japanese at a high level in a wide variety of situations; near-native comprehension and expression.

ko-KR.name: Korean (Korea)
ko-KR.authority: National Institute for International Education, Korea — topik.go.kr (free descriptors)

1.descriptor: Can carry out basic survival communication using mostly familiar words and phrases.

2.descriptor: Can communicate in familiar social situations; limited complexity; basic reading and writing.

3.descriptor: Can use language fairly fluently for most everyday and some professional topics.

4.descriptor: Can use language in professional, academic and political contexts with precision.

5.descriptor: Can use Korean naturally and fluently without limitation; near-native proficiency.

vi-VN.name: Vietnamese (Vietnam)
vi-VN.authority: American Council on the Teaching of Foreign Languages — actfl.org (free guidelines)
vi-VN.note: No single globally standardized Vietnamese proficiency test for Vietnamese as L1/L2. ACTFL is the most widely used free reference.

1.descriptor: Can communicate minimally on very familiar topics using memorized Vietnamese words and phrases.

2.descriptor: Can create simple Vietnamese sentences; handles basic everyday topics with classifiers and tones.

3.descriptor: Can narrate and describe in Vietnamese using paragraph-length discourse; handles tonal precision and classifiers correctly.

4.descriptor: Can discuss topics extensively in Vietnamese; precision in tone marking, classifiers, and formal register.

5.descriptor: Native-like Vietnamese command; can write across formal and informal registers with full tonal and syntactic control.

th-TH.name: Thai (Thailand)
th-TH.authority: American Council on the Teaching of Foreign Languages — actfl.org (free guidelines)
th-TH.note: Thai is tonal and uses classifiers. Politeness particles (ครับ/ค่ะ) indicate gender and register — inconsistency is a non-native marker.

1.descriptor: Can communicate minimally in Thai using memorized words and basic phrases; limited tonal control.

2.descriptor: Can handle uncomplicated communicative tasks in Thai; basic classifier and particle use.

3.descriptor: Can narrate and describe in Thai using paragraph-length discourse with consistent tonal representation.

4.descriptor: Can discuss topics extensively in Thai with precision; correct use of politeness levels and classifiers.

5.descriptor: Native-like Thai command with full tonal, classifier and register control across formal and informal contexts.

ms-MY.name: Malay (Malaysia)
ms-MY.authority: American Council on the Teaching of Foreign Languages — actfl.org (free guidelines)
ms-MY.note: Malaysian Standard Malay (Bahasa Malaysia). Avoid rojak (English-Malay code-switching) in formal register.

1.descriptor: Can communicate minimally in Malay using memorized words and phrases.

2.descriptor: Can handle basic communicative tasks in Malay; simple imbuhan (affixation) use.

3.descriptor: Can narrate and describe in Malay with correct imbuhan and active/passive voice distinction.

4.descriptor: Can discuss topics extensively in Malay; precise imbuhan use, formal register without rojak.

5.descriptor: Native-like Malay command; correct imbuhan throughout, formal vs colloquial register control.

id-ID.name: Indonesian (Indonesia)
id-ID.authority: Badan Pengembangan dan Pembinaan Bahasa, Indonesia — badanbahasa.kemdikbud.go.id (free)
id-ID.note: UKBI (Uji Kemahiran Berbahasa Indonesia) is the official standardized test, administered by the Indonesian government.

1.descriptor: Very limited Indonesian proficiency; frequent errors impede communication even in simple contexts.

2.descriptor: Can handle basic everyday Indonesian communication; some errors in imbuhan and active/passive voice.

3.descriptor: Can communicate on a range of topics with reasonable accuracy; handles most imbuhan patterns correctly.

4.descriptor: Can use Indonesian effectively in professional and formal contexts; consistent use of bahasa baku.

5.descriptor: Master-level Indonesian; precise, fluent and culturally appropriate across all formal and informal registers.
